title: the public drawer
html:
this is the part of the notebook anyone can read. nothing precious lives here — just the things that don't need a key.

everything else stays in the locked drawer below. not because it's secret, just because it's quieter that way.

title: updates on the current projects
html:
finally moved pixlings to paused. it's a formality more than anything — i haven't touched it in a month or two and i'm focused on other things now. the status is just me admitting it out loud.

a few wip updates while i'm here. slab has probably had a new release by the time you read this, hopefully with a few critical bugs squashed. glypnik is getting attention too, but mostly performance work, not features.

i'm also poking at two new app ideas. the first is called clips — a fork of the clipboard side of slab. shizuku-based background capture, a clean interface to sort and store things. somewhere mid-development i realised i was building my own version of nothing's essential space. didn't mean to. just happened.

the second is a gallery app, loosely based on the nothing os preview drop — the one with the subject-isolation thumbnail thing. both feel snappy and good to use, which is the part i care about. no idea when or if these go public, so don't get attached.

title: some new ideas and 1 random one
html:
quick one. the slab update is out. i'm currently trying to fold clips into slab as part of it — basic integration works, by which i mean it works maybe one time in ten.

in the next post (or whenever i get around to it) i'll put together a list of every project with an actual download link, so people can install them themselves.

two ideas i've been turning over. the first has been sitting in my head for a while — a glyph matrix toy with gestures. press the glyph button, wave or shake the phone, trigger actions. moto-gestures style, but on the matrix. no idea if it's even possible.

the second came to me yesterday and is probably less plausible. the new 4a glyph bar as a light-painting tool. long-exposure photography at night, friend with a camera, glyph bar drawing text or shapes through the air. the kind of thing that probably doesn't work but is fun to think about.

title: the fork, and the list i promised
html:
an app i used personally broke sometime last year. didn't realise for ages that nobody else had picked it up to fix — the upstream repo just sat there, untouched. so i forked it. carrier vanity name, if you want the project page.

the fork works on my device — nothing phone (3), nos 4.1, android 16. i can't guarantee anything for other phones or os builds, but i'll try to keep it working as long as my own phone keeps wanting it. that's the whole maintenance promise.

i'd upstream the patch, but the upstream repo looks pretty dead tbh — and honestly, leaving the fix in a small fork means it doesn't suddenly become a thing people install in droves. quieter that way. no influx of users, no expectations.

anyway. as promised — here are the projects with an actual download link or source repo, so you can grab them yourself:

  • carrier vanity name · apk · source
  • glyphclocks · apk · source
  • slab · apk
  • glyph sharge · source
  • glyphcade · source
  • thinking of you · source

title: !thing widgets — a hack, not a product
html:
spent a while reverse-engineering the surface nothing's own widgets register through — a private app called com.nothing.cardservice that sits between widget providers and the launcher / lockscreen / aod hosts. hooked into it. now a few custom widgets appear in the picker and render on all three surfaces. the system treats them as stock.

all credit to martmists, who got there first. that's what made this possible at all — without their work i'd never have known the cardservice surface existed.

this is an undocumented api. only tested on nothing os 4.1, phone (3). it will probably break in future os updates. i'm not going to pretend otherwise, and i'm not going to call it a product. it lives on its own page rather than the projects grid for that reason — !thing widgets, if you want to read about it.

i'll maybe post an apk soon. no promises on timing, no promises on fixing it when it breaks. quieter that way.

title: chromext, and a widget update
html:
forked chromext and got it working on the new naver whale browser versions. probably works on chrome and the other chromium browsers too — didn't test, didn't really care to. only updated it because i wanted it for myself.

not planning to post or host this one.

on the widget side — not sure i mentioned this last time, but i've got a stock music widget clone working on lockscreen now. still buggy in spots. overall happy with the outcome.

one more left. fork the photo widget, turn it into a shortcut — then (nearly) every kwgt widget i was running has a !thing equivalent. that'll be the lot.
